intro.title: Opening files
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Use `open(path, mode)` — modes include r, w, a, rb, wb. Always close files or use `with`.

File objects read/write text or bytes depending on mode and encoding.

Paths can be strings or pathlib.Path objects.
intro.sandboxCode:
from pathlib import Path
p = Path("notes.txt")
# with p.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
#     f.write("hello")
print(p.name, p.suffix)

intro.title: Reading files
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`.read()` whole file; `.readline()` one line; iterate `for line in f` memory-friendly.

`.readlines()` returns list of lines. Strip newlines when processing.

Large files: loop lines instead of read() all at once.

#     for line in f:
#         print(line.strip())

print(text.splitlines())

intro.title: Writing files
intro.bodyMarkdown:
`.write(string)` writes text; must include newlines yourself. `writelines` writes iterable.

w mode overwrites; a appends. Use with block.

Flush ensures data hits disk before close in some cases.
intro.sandboxCode:

# with open("out.txt", "w") as f:
#     f.writelines(lines)
print("".join(lines))

intro.title: Appending
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Mode `a` opens for append — writes go to end without truncating.

Create file if missing. Combine with newline-terminated lines for logs.

Read-append-update patterns need careful design or a+ mode.

intro.title: with statement
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Context managers guarantee setup/cleanup — files close even on exceptions.

`with open(...) as f:` is the standard pattern.

Implement __enter__/__exit__ for custom resources.

challenges.title: Easy: with open
challenges.starterCode:
with open("x","w") as f:
    pass

challenges.title: Medium: nested with
challenges.starterCode:
from io import StringIO
a=""; b=""
with StringIO("a") as f1:
 a=f1.read()
 with StringIO("b") as f2:
  b=f2.read()
v=a+b

challenges.title: Medium: exception still closes
challenges.starterCode:
class C:
 def __init__(self): self.closed=False
 def __enter__(self): return self
 def __exit__(self,*a): self.closed=True
c=C()
try:
 with c:
  raise ValueError
except ValueError:
 pass
closed=c.closed

challenges.title: Expert: __enter__ returns

class C:
 def __enter__(self): return 1
 def __exit__(self,*a): pass
with C() as v: pass

challenges.title: Expert: __exit__ cleanup
challenges.starterCode:
class CM:
 def __enter__(self): return self
 def __exit__(self,*a):
  global cleaned
  cleaned=True
with CM():
 pass

intro.title: File paths
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Use pathlib.Path for cross-platform paths. Join with / operator: `base / "data" / "file.txt"`.

os.path still common; Path has read_text, write_text, exists, mkdir.

Avoid hard-coding backslashes on Windows.
intro.sandboxCode:
from pathlib import Path
base = Path("project")
file = base / "data" / "scores.csv"
print(file.parent, file.name, file.suffix)

intro.title: CSV files
intro.bodyMarkdown:
csv module: reader, writer, DictReader, DictWriter for tabular data.

Comma-separated; handle quoting and headers. Not for Excel-only features.

Always specify newline="" on open in csv docs pattern for Windows.

from io import StringIO

intro.title: JSON files
intro.bodyMarkdown:
json.loads/dumps for strings; load/dump for file objects. Maps to dict/list.

Great for config and APIs. Not all Python types serializable (datetime needs custom).

Pretty-print with indent=2 for readability.
intro.sandboxCode:
import json
data = {"name": "Ada", "scores": [10, 20]}
text = json.dumps(data, indent=2)
back = json.loads(text)
print(back["name"], len(back["scores"]))

challenges.title: Expert: custom encoder

intro.title: Text vs binary
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Text modes expect str with encoding; binary modes use bytes — no decoding.

Images, PDFs, pickles use rb/wb. Never decode arbitrary binary as UTF-8.

b"hello" is bytes literal; encode()/decode() convert str↔bytes.
intro.sandboxCode:
text = "hi"
b = text.encode("utf-8")
print(b, b.decode("utf-8"))
print(type(b).__name__)

intro.title: Error handling with files
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Expect FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, IsADirectoryError. Use try/except around IO.

Validate paths before open; provide friendly messages.

Finally/with ensures cleanup even on errors.

intro.title: Medium project: Todo list app
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Store tasks in a list of dicts: `{id, text, done}`. Support add, list, complete, and delete.

Persist to JSON file on quit; load on start. Use functions for each command.

CLI menu loop: print options, read choice, dispatch action.

intro.title: Hard project: Contact book
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Dict keyed by name or list with search. Fields: name, phone, email.

Commands: add, find, update, delete. Save JSON to disk.

Normalize phone digits for comparison; validate email contains @.
